reasoning: `set_map_union` is `external_body` with `unimplemented!()` — the distributive law is provable via `assert_sets_equal!` but is instead trusted as an axiom, creating an unnecessary soundness dependency.
Empty set is the identity for union, so `(∅ + s).map(f) == s.map(f)` follows from the distributive law plus `∅.map(f) == ∅`. Standard set algebra.
`s + s == s` for sets, so `(s + s).map(f) == s.map(f)` is a direct consequence of union idempotence. Correct property.
Standard monotonicity: `s1 ⊆ s1 ∪ s2` implies `s1.map(f) ⊆ (s1 ∪ s2).map(f)`. Correct consequence of the distributive law.
Three-way distribution follows from applying the two-way law twice, combined with set union associativity. The auto quantifier correctly chains.
